docs.title: The Vampire Tracker API: Immortal Data Tracked Instantly
docs.text: The Vampire Tracker API (Vampy API) provides a structured, searchable, and canonical data source for the notable vampires across film, television, literature, and lore. Built using json-server for deployment simplicity and accessibility, the Vampy API allows developers to access detailed information about individual vampires, their special abilities, and their media appearances.

docs.title: Vampy API benefits
docs.text:
The Vampy API provides a reliable endpoint to query the characteristics and cinematic history of legendary vampires. The Vampy API provides the following benefits:

  • The Vampy API data model ensures that your application's facts about vampires are consistent and accurate.

  • The Vampy API leverages a standard REST architecture and clear JSON response, allowing you to quickly recite popular vampires in minutes.

  • The relationship structure allows for expansion, letting you link powers, media, and other attributes to create an accurate vampire profile.

docs.title: Vampy topics
docs.text:
This website is organized into three primary sections that support developers during all stages of integration.

Section Purpose Topics covered Getting started Used to get up and running in under five minutes. Installation, First API call using GET. Reference Provides comprehensive detail on all resources and operations Resources schema, endpoints, methods, filtering, and sorting. Tutorials Provides step-by-step instructions for common tasks related to the Vampy API Add new vampire profiles, filtering vampires by power.

docs.title: Resource references
docs.text: The Vampy API exposes three primary, interconnected resources. The core of the database is the vampires resources. The vampires resource links to the media and specialPowers resource via the vampireId key.

docs.title: Ready to sink your teeth in?
docs.text: Start by reading the Quick Start guide to make your first API call. Then, explore the Reference guide for advanced querying.

docs.title: Becoming familiar: Getting started with the Vampy API
docs.text:
This tutorial guides new users through the essential steps required to run the Vampire Tracker API (Vampy API) locally using json-server. By the end of this tutorial, you will have the REST API running and accessible at http://localhost:3000.

Expect this preparation to take about 20 minutes to complete.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before starting, ensure that you meet the following prerequisites:

  • Node.js, which is used for running JavaScript packages.
  • json-server, which is used to host our API endpoints from the JSON database file.
  • git command line tool, which is used to clone the repository.

docs.title: Getting the project files
docs.text:
The following procedure shows you how to clone the Github repository.

  1. Open a terminal. Ensure that you are in the $HOME directory by running the following command:

    
cd $HOME

  2. Clone the project by running the following command:

    
git clone https://github.com/stevsmit/vampy-api.git

docs.title: Staring the Vampy API server
docs.text:
The following procedure shows you how to start the Vampy API data.

  1. Navigate into the vampy-api/api directory by running the following command:

    
cd vampy-api/api

  2. Start the JSON server by running the following command:

    
json-server vampy_db.json

    Your terminal will show confirmation messages that the server is running and the available resources:

    \{^_^}/ hi!

Loading vampy_db.json
Done

Resources
http://localhost:3000/vampires
http://localhost:3000/media
http://localhost:3000/specialPowers

Home
http://localhost:3000

Type s + enter at any time to create a snapshot of the database

docs.title: Making a test call to the Vampy API server
docs.text:
The following procedure shows you how to make a test call to the Vampy API server.

  1. Open a different terminal window, or a new terminal.

  2. Retrieve information for the /vampire resource by entering the following command. By default, running the curl command automatically retrieves resources with the GET.

    
curl http://localhost:3000/vampires

    If the service is running, information about the /vampires resource is returned. For example:

    [
{
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Selene",
    "canFly": false,
    "batMode": false
},
{
    "id": 2,
    "name": "Peter Loew",
    "canFly": false,
    "batMode": false
},
{
    "id": 3,
    "name": "David",
    "canFly": true,
    "batMode": false
},
{
    "id": 4,
    "name": "Strigoi",
    "canFly": true,
    "batMode": true
},
{
    "id": 5,
    "name": "Count Dracula",
    "canFly": true,
    "batMode": true
},
{
    "id": 6,
    "name": "The Count",
    "canFly": true,
    "batMode": true
},
{
    "id": 7,
    "name": "Blade",
    "canFly": false,
    "batMode": false
},
]

docs.title: About the /media resource
docs.text: The /media resource represents a media appearance associated with a vampire in the Vampy API. Each entry includes the title of the media, its release year, and a foreign key (vampireId) linking the appearance to a specific vampire. You can create, read, update, or delete media entries using the corresponding API endpoints.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Example id integer Unique identifier for this entry. 1 type string The medium where the vampire appears (for example, movie, television, book, etc.) movie title string The title of the work "Underworld" vampireId integer Links to the specific vampire in the vampires resource. 1 year integer The year of the media's release or publication. 2003

docs.title: Related endpoints
docs.text: Path Description GET /media Retrieve a list of all media entries. GET /media/{id} Retrieve a single media entry by its id. GET /media?vampireId={id} Retrieve all media entries linked to a specific vampire. POST /media Retrieve a vampire profile along with its assigned special powers. PATCH /media/{id} Update one or more fields of an existing media entry. PUT /media/{id} Replace an existing media entry with a new object (all fields required). DELETE /media/{id} Delete a media entry.

docs.title: About the /vampires resource
docs.text: The /vampires resource represents a vampire profile in the Vampy API. Each entry contains the vampire's name, abilities, and unique identifier. This resource is central to link media appearances and special powers. You can create, read, update, or delete vampire profiles using the corresponding API endpoints.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Example id integer Unique identifier for the vampire 1 name string The common name or title of the vampire. "Selene" canFly boolean Indicates whether the vampire is capable of flight in their base form. true batMode boolean Indicates whether the vampire can transform into a bat. false

docs.title: Related endpoints
docs.text: Path Description GET /vampires Retrieve a list of all vampire profiles. GET /vampires/{id} Retrieve a single vampire profile by its id. GET /vampires?canFly={true/false} Retrieve all vampires that can or cannot fly. GET /vampires/{id}?_embed=specialPowers Retrieve a vampire profile along with its assigned special powers. GET /vampires/{id}?_embed=media Retrieve a single vampire profile and embed all associated media appearances in the response. POST /vampires Create a new vampire profile. Requires name, canFly, and batMode. PATCH /vampires/{id} Update one or more fields on an existing vampire. PUT /vampires/{id} Replace an existing vampire with a new object (all fields required). DELETE /vampires/{id} Delete a vampire profile.

docs.title: DELETE /media references
docs.text: Use the DELETE method to remove a media entry.

docs.title: DELETE /media/{id}
docs.text: Delete one media resource by ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) Unique ID of the media. integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Delete successful {} 404 Media not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X DELETE http://{base_url}/media/3

docs.title: DELETE /vampires references
docs.text: Use the DELETE method to delete a /vampire resource.

docs.title: DELETE /vampires/{id}
docs.text: Delete a vampire by its ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) Unique ID of the vampire integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Vampire profile successfully deleted Vampire object 404 Vampire not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X DELETE http://{base_url}/vampires/1

docs.title: GET /media references
docs.text: Use the GET method to read one or more /media resources.

docs.title: GET /media
docs.text: Retrieve all media entries.

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 A list of media entries Media[] 500 Server error ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET http://{base_url}/media

docs.title: GET /media/{id}
docs.text: Retrieve a single media entry by ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) The media ID integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Media successfully retrieved Media 404 Media not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET http://{base_url}/media/1

docs.title: GET /media?vampireId={id}
docs.text: Retrieve all media appearances associated with a particular vampire.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type vampireId (required) Returns only media items linked to the specified vampire integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 A filtered list of media entries Media[] 400 Invalid query parameter ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET "http://{base_url}/media?vampireId=3"

docs.title: GET /vampires references
docs.text: Use the GET method to retrieve information about the /vampire resource.

docs.title: GET /vampires
docs.text: Retrieve a list of all vampire profiles.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type None Retrieve all vampires -

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 List of all vampire profiles array of Vampires

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" http://{base_url}/vampires

docs.title: GET /vampires/{id}
docs.text: Retrieve a single vampire profile by its unique id.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id Unique ID of the vampire integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Vampire profile Vampire object 404 Vampire not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" http://{base_url}/vampires/1

docs.title: GET /vampires?canFly={true/false}
docs.text: Retrieve all vampires that are, or are not, capable of flight.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type canFly Filter vampires by flight capability boolean

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 List of vampires that can fly array of Vampire objects

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" http://{base_url}/vampires?canFly=true

docs.title: GET /vampires/{id}?_embed=specialPowers
docs.text: Retrieve a single vampire profile with its special powers.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id Unique ID of the vampire integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Vampire profile with embedded special powers Vampire object with specialPowers array 404 Vampire not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text: curl -X GET -H "Content-Type: application/json" http://{base_url}/vampires/1?_embed=specialPowers

docs.title: GET /vampires/{id}?_embed=media
docs.text: Retrieve a single vampire profile and embed all associated media appearances in the response.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) Unique ID of the vampire integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Vampire profile with embedded special powers Vampire object with specialPowers array 404 Vampire not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X GET \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "http://{base_url}/vampires/1?_embed=media"

docs.title: API reference: REST operations
docs.text:
The Vampy API supports standard RESTful operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) on all three primary resources: /vampires, /media, and /specialPowers. All requests and responses are handled via JSON.

The following sections provide an overview of each operation.

docs.title: Read operations (GET)
docs.text:
Use GET to retrieve Vampy API resources.

Operation Endpoint example Description Get all GET /vampires Retrieve a list of all vampire profiles. Get by ID GET /vampires/7 Retrieve the profile for the vampire with the 7 id (Blade). Filter GET /vampires?canFly=true Retrieve all vampires that can fly. Relational data GET /specialPowers?_expand=vampire Retrieve all special powers, embedding the associated vampire object with the response for each power.

Example request (GET /vampires):

curl -X GET \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  http://localhost:3000/vampires

Example response (GET /vampires):

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Selene",
    "canFly": false,
    "batMode": false
  },
  {
    "id": 7,
    "name": "Blade",
    "canFly": false,
    "batMode": false
  }
  // ... more vampires
]

docs.title: Create operations (POST)
docs.text:
Use POST to create new resources. The API automatically assigns a unique id to the new entry upon success.

Endpoint Description Required fields POST /vampires Adds a new vampire profile. name, canFly (boolean), batMode (boolean) POST /media Records a new media appearance for an existing vampire. type, title, year, vampireiD (integer) POST /specialPowers Assigns a new power to an existing vampire. power, vampireId (integer)

Example request (POST /vampires):

curl -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Count Orlok", "canFly": false, "batMode": false}' \
  http://localhost:3000/vampires

Example response if successful:

{
  "name": "Count Orlok",
  "canFly": false,
  "batMode": false,
  "id": 8
}

docs.title: Delete operations (DELETE)
docs.text:
Use DELETE to remove a resource from the database by using its id.

Example Request (DELETE /media/3):

curl -X DELETE http://localhost:3000/media/3

Example response if successful:

{}

docs.title: PATCH /media references
docs.text: Use the PATCH method to update one or more fields of an existing media entry.

docs.title: PATCH /media/{id}
docs.text: Update partial fields on an existing media object.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
At least one field must be provided.

Parameter Description Type id (required) The media ID integer title (optional) New title of the media string year (optional) Updated release year integer vampireId (optional) Associated vampire ID integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Update successful Media 400 Invalid update payload ApiError 404 Media not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X PATCH \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"year": 2010}' \
  http://{base_url}/media/1

docs.title: PATCH /vampires/{id} references
docs.text: Use the PATCH method to update one or more fields on an existing vampire using the vampire's ID. Only the fields included in the request will be updated. Other fields remain unchanged.

docs.title: PATCH /vampires/{id}
docs.text: Update the vampire profile.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) Unique ID of the vampire integer name (optional) Friendly name of the vampire string canFly (optional) Whether the vampire can fly boolean batMode (optional) Whether the vampire can transform into a bat boolean

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 201 Successful creation Vampire object 400 Bad request ApiError 404 Vampire not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X PATCH \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"canFly": true}' \
  http://{base_url}/vampires/1

docs.title: POST /media references
docs.text: Use the POST method to create a new /media resource.

docs.title: POST /media
docs.text: Create a new media entry associated with a vampire.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type title (required) Title of the media work string year (required) Release year integer vampireId (required) ID of the vampire featured in the media integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 201 Successful creation Media object 400 Bad request ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title": "Let the Right One In", "year": 2008, "vampireId": 2}' \
  http://{base_url}/media

docs.title: POST /vampires references
docs.text: Use the POST method to create a new /vampire resource.

docs.title: POST /vampires
docs.text: Create a new vampire profile.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type name (required) Friendly name of the vampire string canFly (required) Whether the vampire can fly boolean batMode (required) Whether the vampire can transform into a bat boolean

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 201 Successful creation Vampire object 400 Bad request ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Count Orlok", "canFly": false, "batMode": false}' \
  http://{base_url}/vampires

docs.title: PUT /media references
docs.text: Use the PUT method to replace an existing /media resource with a new object.

docs.title: PUT /media/{id}
docs.text: Replace an entire media entry. All fields are required.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) The media ID integer title (required) New title of the media string year (required) Updated release year integer vampireId (required) Associated vampire ID integer

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 200 Replacement creation Media 400 Missing or invalid fields ApiError 404 Media not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X PUT \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id": 1, "title": "Underworld (Director’s Cut)", "year": 2003, "vampireId": 1}' \
  http://{base_url}/media/1

docs.title: PUT /vampires/{id} references
docs.text: Use the PUT method to replace an existing vampire with a new object. All fields must be included in the request. Missing fields will be set to null or default values.

docs.title: PUT /vampires/{id}
docs.text: Update the entire vampire profile.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Type id (required) Unique ID of the vampire integer name (required) Friendly name of the vampire string canFly (required) Whether the vampire can fly boolean batMode (required) Whether the vampire can transform into a bat boolean

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: HTTP Code Description Schema 201 Successful creation Vampire object 400 Bad request ApiError 404 Vampire not found ApiError

docs.title: Example command
docs.text:
curl -X PUT \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Selene", "canFly": false, "batMode": false, "id": 1}' \
  http://{base_url}/vampires/1

docs.title: The /specialPowers resource
docs.text: Property Type Description Example id integer Unique identifier for the power entry. 1 power string The name or description of the specific power. "Day walking" vampireId integer Links to the specific vampire in the /vampires resource. 7

docs.title: Tutorial: Creating a complete vampire profile
docs.text:
This tutorial shows you how to use the POST method to add a new vampire entity to the database, along with their media appearance and special power.

This tutorial should take approximately 20 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: • Confirm that the json-server app is running with the vampy_db.json database. For more information, see Becoming familiar: Getting started with the Vampy API.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Open a terminal.

  2. Run the following cURL command using the POST method. This command creates the core entry to the /vampires resource.

    
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{
    "name": "Edward Cullen",
    "canFly": false,
    "batMode": false
}' http://localhost:3000/vampires

    The API returns the new vampire object, which includes a unique id assigned by the server. For example:

    {
"name": "Edward Cullen",
"canFly": false,
"batMode": false,
"id": 8
}

  3. Run the following cURL command using the POST method to add the movie Twilight to the /media resource. Include the vampireId generated from Step 2.

    
curl -X POST \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{
    "type": "film", 
    "title": "Twilight",
    "year": 2008,
    "vampireId": 8
}' \
http://localhost:3000/media

    The API returns the new power object with its own unique id. For example:

    {
"type": "film",
"title": "Twilight",
"year": 2008,
"vampireId": 8,
"id": 5
}

  4. Run the following cURL command using the POST method to add the power Enhanced Speed to the /specialPowers resource. Include the vampireId generated from Step 2.

    
curl -X POST \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{
    "power": "Enhanced Speed", 
    "vampireId": 8
}' \
http://localhost:3000/specialPowers

    The API returns the new power object with its own unique id. For example:

    {
"power": "Enhanced Speed",
"vampireId": 9,
"id": 8
}

docs.title: Completion and validation
docs.text:
1. Run the following cURL command using the GET method to confirm the changes:

    curl -X GET \
http://localhost:3000/vampires/8?_embed=specialPowers

    The response includes the base vampire object with an array of powers:

    {
"name": "Edward Cullen",
"canFly": false,
"batMode": false,
"id": 9,
"specialPowers": [
    {
    "power": "Enhanced Speed",
    "vampireId": 9,
    "id": 8
    }
]
}
